business_information.what_products_or_services_do_you_offer:
Prairie Harvest Artisan Bakery is a small-batch, scratch bakery specializing in naturally leavened sourdough breads, European-style pastries, and a rotating selection of seasonal baked goods that highlight Alberta-grown grains and local ingredients.

Our core product lines include:
- Breads: country sourdough loaves, multigrain sandwich loaves, rye sourdough, focaccia, baguettes, and weekly specialty loaves (e.g., roasted garlic & rosemary, cranberry walnut, cheddar jalapeño). All of our breads are made with long-fermentation techniques and primarily Canadian-grown flours.

- Pastries: butter croissants, pain au chocolat, morning buns, brioche buns, cinnamon rolls, fruit galettes, lemon tartlets, and seasonal items like pumpkin scones in the fall or rhubarb danishes in the spring. We also do a small line of gluten-friendly (not celiac-certified) items like almond flour brownies and flourless chocolate cake slices.

- Cakes & Special Orders: custom buttercream cakes for birthdays and small events (typically 6" to 10" round cakes), cupcake assortments, dessert platters for office meetings, and holiday pre-order boxes (e.g., Christmas cookie boxes, Easter brunch boxes, Thanksgiving pie packages). We don’t do highly intricate fondant work; our style is more rustic and natural, with fresh flowers and seasonal fruit.

- Coffee & Beverages: we partner with a local Edmonton roaster to serve drip coffee, espresso-based drinks (lattes, cappuccinos, americanos), and a small selection of teas and house-made syrups. The café side of the bakery is modest but designed as a cozy neighborhood spot with about 12 seats.

- Wholesale & Corporate: we supply a handful of nearby independent coffee shops with sourdough loaves and pastries three times a week, and we also provide breakfast pastry trays and sandwich platters for a few local offices and a co-working space, mostly on a pre-order basis.

We place a strong emphasis on using local and ethically sourced ingredients: free-range eggs, local dairy, and flours milled in Western Canada when possible. Everything is made on-site in our open kitchen so customers can see the baking in progress. We also occasionally host small weekend workshops on sourdough bread baking for home bakers, which we’d like to expand in the future.
business_information.who_is_your_typical_customer_or_client:
Our typical customers are a mix of local neighborhood residents, young professionals, and families who value high-quality, handmade food and are willing to pay a bit more for artisanal products.

More specifically:
- Neighborhood Regulars: people who live within a 10–15 minute walk or drive of the bakery in West Edmonton. Many are in the 30–55 age range, often professionals or small business owners themselves. They stop by for their weekly sourdough loaf, weekend pastries, and coffee. Several of them have standing pre-orders for bread (for example, one customer, a nurse at the nearby hospital, picks up two multigrain loaves every Thursday after her shift).

- Young Professionals & Remote Workers: individuals in their 20s and 30s who work from home or in nearby co-working spaces. They tend to come in mid-morning or mid-afternoon for a latte and a pastry, often using the café seating as a quiet place to work for an hour. Many of them follow us on Instagram and discover new items (like our seasonal cruffins) through social media.

- Families: parents with young children who come in on weekends for a treat after visiting the nearby park or farmer’s market. They tend to buy a mix of pastries, cookies, and a loaf of bread for the week. We know several kids by name and often set aside their favorite items (for example, chocolate croissants or cinnamon rolls) when parents message us in advance.

- Health- and Ingredient-Conscious Customers: people who care about where their food comes from, prefer fewer additives and preservatives, and are often looking for long-fermented breads that are easier to digest. We get quite a few customers who have mild sensitivities to commercial bread and specifically seek out sourdough.

- Small Local Businesses & Offices: independent coffee shops, a yoga studio, and a couple of professional offices (accounting and marketing firms) that order wholesale or catering from us. They typically order pastry trays, sandwich platters on our sourdough, and sometimes custom cakes for staff birthdays or client events.

Demographically, our customer base is largely Edmonton-based, mostly English-speaking, but we also see a fair number of newcomers to Canada who are familiar with European-style bakeries and appreciate the sourdough focus. Many customers find us through Instagram, Google Maps, or word-of-mouth in local community Facebook groups. Overall, our typical client values freshness, transparency about ingredients, a friendly and personal connection with the staff, and a cozy, community-oriented atmosphere rather than a chain café experience.
